_meta.provenance: Master question & decision register. Seeded with platform-level questions arising from real gaps between the proposed model and the Indian sports ecosystem. Module-specific banks are authored per module in Phase 2.

questions.question: Does NSRS expose a programmatic API for identity lookup and profile read/write, or is access portal-only? The whole identity backbone depends on this.
questions.impact: Without API access, M7 needs a manual/batch bridge and the 'one identity' promise weakens.

questions.question: Do all states use compatible athlete identifiers, or do state registries carry their own IDs that need mapping to NSRS?
questions.impact: Determines the size of the de-duplication and identity-mapping effort in every data-bearing module.

questions.question: Central grants flow through PFMS — but how will state-funded grants (outside PFMS) be captured for the fund-tracking promise?
questions.impact: Fund traceability would silently exclude a large share of actual sports spending.

questions.question: Who validates district-level facility data, and what happens where a district has no digital source system at all?
questions.impact: Defines the manual-capture fallback and the trust level of the GIS registry.

questions.question: How will duplicate athlete/result records across federations and states be resolved, and which entity's record wins a dispute?
questions.impact: Needs a documented precedence rule before results consolidate into Athlete 360.

questions.question: After an external system integrates, which entity owns ongoing updates and corrections — the source system or KHELO BHARAT?
questions.impact: Systems-of-record must be declared per dataset or data will fork.

questions.question: What is the legally sufficient mechanism for verifiable guardian consent before capturing a minor's screening data under the DPDP Act?
questions.impact: Blocks any school pilot until answered; shapes the School App enrolment flow.

questions.question: Which approvals are mandatory before a co-branded card pilot — NPCI product approval, bank partnership, MYAS financial approval, or all three in sequence?
questions.impact: Determines the critical path for the flagship Benefits-layer module.

questions.question: Which education boards are willing to pilot marks/credit recognition for verified sports achievement, and under what policy instrument (NCrF mapping, board circular, or state order)?
questions.impact: M16 is a policy unlock — the module is inert until at least one board commits.

questions.question: What data can be publicly displayed on the State Excellence Index, and what must remain restricted to government dashboards?
questions.impact: Public ranking of states is politically sensitive; publication rules must precede the index.

questions.question: How far back will historical data (results, grants, assessments) be migrated, and how will unverifiable historical records be labeled?
questions.impact: Defines migration scope and the meaning of verification badges on old data.

questions.question: When two source organisations disagree about a record (e.g., a federation and a state department), what is the resolution forum and SLA?
questions.impact: Without a dispute path, contested records stall verification queues.

questions.question: The concept anchors identity on an 'NSRS ID', but the live NSRS portal issues a 'Khelo India ID (KID)' on registration. Which identifier is canonical for Khelo Bharat, and are they the same thing?
questions.impact: Terminology and key design across every module reference this identifier; it must match the real system.

questions.question: What is the transition timeline for the National Sports Board assuming recognition powers from MYAS, and in what format will the statutory recognition register be published?
questions.impact: Decides whether M2 mirrors an MYAS list (interim) or the NSB register (target), and when the switch happens.

questions.question: Will SAI share sanctioned-post and vacancy data, and may any of it be published beyond government dashboards?
questions.impact: The vacancy view is administratively sensitive; publication scope changes the module's public value.

questions.question: Are state sports-department structures similar enough to fit one national hierarchy schema, or does the directory need per-state structural extensions?
questions.impact: Schema flexibility must be decided before state onboarding begins.

questions.question: Can eOffice expose file-status events for identified files to an external platform — is there any precedent for such an integration?
questions.impact: Without it, 'where is my file' relies on manual desk updates, which decay.

questions.question: Who encodes and maintains scheme eligibility rules as structured criteria — the scheme division, the platform team, or a joint board — and who signs off that the encoding matches the guidelines?
questions.impact: A wrong encoding wrongly rejects (or wrongly promises) benefits at national scale.

questions.question: Does DARPG permit a sports-domain grievance front-end that cross-references CPGRAMS, or must all citizen grievances live wholly inside CPGRAMS with the platform consuming category feeds?
questions.impact: Determines M4's entire architecture — front-end + sync versus embedded workflows.

questions.question: What instrument gives SLA clocks and auto-escalation authority over state and district resolvers, given the centre cannot mandate state service levels?
questions.impact: Without adopted authority, escalations are notifications nobody must act on.

questions.question: What are the handling requirements for safeguarding grievances involving minors — sealed access, mandatory reporting, and interaction with safe-sport mechanisms under the NSGA 2025?
questions.impact: A mis-handled minor-safety case is the worst possible failure of this module; the sealed track must be designed before launch.

questions.question: What share of target schools have a device and connectivity adequate for the offline-first School App, and who provides devices where none exist?
questions.impact: Defines the paper-fallback design and the real cost of national coverage.

questions.question: Is e-Khel Pathshala the training channel for screening assessors, and can scout certification ride its existing courses?
questions.impact: Reuses an existing channel instead of building a training platform.

questions.question: Do screening results enter the child's APAAR co-curricular record, or remain solely in the sports domain?
questions.impact: Changes consent scope, MoE approval content and where the record lives.

questions.question: Is scout recognition non-monetary (certificates, career credit, national honours) or monetary — and if monetary, whose budget pays across central and state teachers?
questions.impact: Monetary rewards trigger financial approvals and state alignment; the answer shapes A-M6-2.

questions.question: When two scouts claim discovery of the same athlete, what resolves it — earliest verified session, or a committee review?
questions.impact: The 'first scouted by' credit must be uncontested to motivate anyone.

questions.question: Are sports-science assessments 'health data' attracting heightened DPDP treatment, and what retention period applies to a minor's assessment history?
questions.impact: Determines the sealed-store design and deletion obligations for D-M7-3.

questions.question: Who audits the talent model for bias across regions, gender and disciplines, on what schedule, and what is the athlete's appeal path against a score?
questions.impact: Without governance, the AI engine is a selection scandal in waiting; shapes A-M7-2.

questions.question: What exactly does SAI's Games Management System (GMS) cover, and is it available (or mandated) for events beyond Khelo India Games?
questions.impact: Draws the M8/GMS boundary — build, extend, or coordinate; shapes A-M8-2.

questions.question: What evidence standard makes a result 'verified' — timing-system export, referee sign-off, both — and does it vary by event level?
questions.impact: Defines the trust level of every result on the Athlete 360 timeline.

questions.question: What is the lawful background-check standard for coaches and scouts working with minors — who runs it, what does it cover, and how often must it renew?
questions.impact: Defines A-M9-3 and the vetting dataset; a safeguarding failure is an existential platform risk.

questions.question: What makes a private-sector coach register — visibility in the M12 marketplace, verified-credential badge, or access to deployment opportunities?
questions.impact: Without private coaches the registry covers only the government cadre.

questions.question: Will PM GatiShakti accept a sports-facility layer, under whose maintenance, and does layer data flow both ways?
questions.impact: Decides whether M10 is a national-planning input or a standalone map; shapes A-M10-1.

questions.question: Where do public booking fees for government facilities go — treasury, facility account, or a revolving maintenance fund?
questions.impact: Booking cannot launch without a lawful fee route; shapes A-M10-3.

questions.question: Does a QR-scan-based register satisfy General Financial Rules stock-verification and write-off requirements, or must paper stock books continue in parallel?
questions.impact: If both run, the digital register loses; A-M11-3 depends on this.

questions.question: How are legacy (untagged) assets enumerated — one-time physical audit, rolling capture at annual stock verification, or new-purchases-only?
questions.impact: Determines when the register reflects reality rather than recent purchases.

questions.question: What do the existing Khelo India academy accreditation standards cover today, how many academies hold accreditation, and are the criteria published?
questions.impact: M12 must extend the existing programme; shapes A-M12-1.

questions.question: What review-integrity mechanism protects academy ratings — verified-enrolment gate, moderation SLAs, academy right of reply, penalties for manipulation?
questions.impact: Rankings carry commercial consequences; shapes A-M12-3.

questions.question: Are athlete injury/treatment records held in ABDM-linked health systems (platform holds pathway state only), or platform-native — and in either case, who is the health-data fiduciary?
questions.impact: The strictest data question on the platform; blocks M13 build until answered.

questions.question: What minimal availability signal may selectors see (available / rehab / cleared) without exposing medical detail, and does the athlete control that visibility?
questions.impact: Balances selection needs against medical privacy; shapes D-M13-4.

questions.question: How does the card work for minors — guardian-linked minor accounts, prepaid construct, or entitlement-only card without a payment function until 18?
questions.impact: Most beneficiaries are school-age; the product construct depends on this.

questions.question: What prevents tier inflation — can only federation-verified, sanctioned-event results trigger tier upgrades, and is there an audit/downgrade path?
questions.impact: Benefits attached to gameable tiers leak money and credibility; shapes A-M14-2.

questions.question: Do sports-quota recruitment rules permit digital verification in lieu of attested certificates, or does each recruiter need a formal rule amendment first?
questions.impact: Blocks A-M15-1 — the module's core promise depends on recruiters legally accepting platform verification.

questions.question: How are retired/transitioning athletes verified when their careers predate the platform — archival verification service, federation attestation, or reduced-confidence labels?
questions.impact: Post-career employment is a headline use case; excluding pre-platform careers guts it.

questions.question: Which achievements qualify for credits — participation, medals, national camps, or graded levels — and who draws that line per education stage?
questions.impact: The mapping (A-M16-2) cannot be drafted without this decision.

questions.question: Marks incentives historically produce certificate fraud — must M16 launch only after the full verification chain (M2 sanctioning + M8 results + DigiLocker certificates) is live?
questions.impact: Sequencing decision that protects the whole framework's credibility.

questions.question: Does exchange-mediated funding qualify under Schedule VII 'promotion of sports', and how do corporates evidence it in statutory CSR disclosures?
questions.impact: Corporate participation hinges on CSR-credit certainty; shapes A-M17-2.

questions.question: Does the platform ever hold or route money (escrow), or is it strictly matchmaking + verified tracking with funds flowing corporate → implementing agency directly?
questions.impact: Blocks M17 build — custodial roles change the legal, audit and corruption-risk profile entirely.

questions.question: What guardian-consent standard governs publicly showcasing under-18 athletes — and what is the takedown SLA when consent is withdrawn?
questions.impact: Most rising athletes are minors; publicity consent must be separate and revocable; shapes A-M18-1.

questions.question: May sponsors reuse platform showcase content commercially, and does the athlete share in any revenue (name-image-likeness treatment)?
questions.impact: Defines usage-rights metadata on every media asset and avoids exploiting athletes' images.

questions.question: How are states with low digital adoption scored — completeness-weighted indicators, provisional bands, or exclusion until a data floor is met?
questions.impact: An index that punishes weak data (not weak sport) destroys state cooperation; shapes A-M19-1.

questions.question: Who operationally runs the command centre — an MYAS cell, SAI, or NIC — with what standing access to cross-module data?
questions.impact: Shapes A-M19-3 and the governance model's analyst roles.

questions.question: What aggregation floor and k-anonymity threshold prevent re-identifying individuals (especially rural minor athletes) in 'anonymised' extracts?
questions.impact: Blocks any external data sharing — one re-identification ends the programme; shapes A-M20-1.

questions.question: How are pro-bono partner teams security-vetted and access-scoped (the concept's evaluation case #1) — and who can revoke access, how fast?
questions.impact: Shapes A-M20-2 and the audit log requirements.
